mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payroll employment indicates a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed periods, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates significantly and remains elevated, it signals an environment of eroding purchasing power and potential monetary tightening, which negatively impacts corporate profitability and equity valuations, leading to lower S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, during periods where the trigger is active, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicate broad-based consumer confidence and investment activity, signaling a healthy demand-side economy that supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between the combined strength of RSAFS and HOUST and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and increased economic uncertainty, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the past 3 months
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following an active trigger, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strong manufacturing activity and business investment, reflecting robust supply-side conditions that translate into higher corporate revenues and positive short-term equity market sentiment, driving S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate indicates a tightening monetary policy stance, which raises borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, reduces liquidity, and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or the Rank IC is below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and consistent GDP growth signifies a robust overall economy with expanding demand, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving equity markets higher over a longer, quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when the GDP growth trigger is active, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth and a stable or declining unemployment rate signifies a healthy and tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive short-term equity returns over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 100,000 AND UNRATE 3-month change < 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where both labor market conditions are met, this hypothesis is rejected.
